```typescript
    order: number,
    createdAt: Date,
  }],
  gameHistory: [{
    id: string,
    eventId: string,
    eventName: string,
    dateTime: Date,
    status: string,
    teamsSnapshot: object,
  }],
  settings: {
    locale: string,
    theme: string,
  },
  exportedAt: Date,
}
```

#### GET /api/users/[id]/export?format=csv

Export data in CSV format (multiple files zipped).

### Export Formats

1. **JSON** (Default)
   - Single file with all data
   - Structured, nested format
   - Easy to parse programmatically

2. **CSV** (Optional)
   - Multiple CSV files zipped together
   - One file per data type (events, players, history)
   - User-friendly for spreadsheet apps

3. **PDF** (Future Enhancement)
   - Human-readable report
   - Summary of stored data
   - Could be used for legal purposes

## Implementation

### Export Flow

```
User clicks "Export My Data"
        ↓
API authenticates user
        ↓
Gather all user data from tables:
  - User
  - Event (owned + participated)
  - Player (linked to account)
  - GameHistory (participated)
  - WebhookSubscription (if any)
        ↓
Format as JSON
        ↓
Return as downloadable file
        ↓
Log export request (GDPR audit)
```

### File Naming Convention

```
export_<userId>_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<timestamp>.json
export_<userId>_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<timestamp>.zip (for CSV)
```

### Response Headers

```http
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="export_abc123_2024-03-19_123456.json"
Cache-Control: no-store
```

## Tasks

- [ ] Create API route `GET /api/users/[id]/export`
- [ ] Implement data gathering logic
- [ ] Format response as JSON
- [ ] Add CSV export support (optional)
- [ ] Rate limit exports (max 5 per day)
- [ ] Add "Export Data" UI to profile
- [ ] Add export progress indicator (for large datasets)
- [ ] Add i18n strings (en, pt, es, fr, de, it)
- [ ] Write tests for export flow
- [ ] Log export requests for GDPR compliance
- [ ] Email notification on export (optional)

### Prisma Queries

```typescript
// Gather all user data
const userData = await prisma.user.findUnique({
  where: { id },
  include: {
    events: {
      include: {
        players: { where: { userId: id } },
        gameHistory: true,
      }
    },
    players: {
      where: { userId },
      include: { event: true }
    },
    webhookSubscriptions: { where: { userId: id } },
  }
});

// Sanitize and format
```

## Security Considerations

1. **Authentication Required**
   - Only authenticated users can export their own data
   - Prevents unauthorized data access

2. **Rate Limiting**
   - Max 5 exports per day
   - Prevents abuse and server overload

3. **No Sensitive Data**
   - Don't include password hashes
   - Don't include auth tokens
   - Don't include other users' private data

4. **Audit Logging**
   - Log when user requests export
   - Store timestamp and IP address
   - Required for GDPR compliance

5. **Data Integrity**
   - Export snapshot is point-in-time
   - May not include real-time updates during export

## Test Cases

```typescript
describe("GET /api/users/[id]/export", () => {
  it("should return JSON export for authenticated user");
  it("should include all user events");
  it("should include all player records");
  it("should include game history");
  it("should not include other users' data");
  it("should not include password hashes");
  it("should rate limit exports (5 per day)");
  it("should return 401 for unauthenticated requests");
  it("should return 403 for exporting other users' data");
  it("should set correct Content-Disposition header");
  it("should work with empty data (new users)");
});
```

## Database Performance

For users with large amounts of data:
- [ ] Add pagination for large datasets
- [ ] Consider streaming response for large exports
- [ ] Cache exports temporarily (optional)
- [ ] Background job processing (for large datasets)

## Example Export File

```json
{
  "export": {
    "version": "1.0",
    "exportedAt": "2024-03-19T12:00:00Z",
    "format": "json"
  },
  "user": {
    "id": "abc123",
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "createdAt": "2023-01-15T10:00:00Z"
  },
  "events": [
    {
      "id": "evt1",
      "title": "Tuesday Football",
      "location": "Riverside Pitch",
      "dateTime": "2024-03-19T18:00:00Z",
      "sport": "football-5v5",
      "teamOneName": "Ninjas",
      "teamTwoName": "Gunas",
      "players": [...]
    }
  ],
  "players": [...],
  "gameHistory": [...],
  "settings": {
    "locale": "en",
    "theme": "dark"
  }
}
```

## Related Files
- `src/pages/api/users/[id]/export.ts` (new)
- `src/components/ProfilePage.tsx` (UI)
- `src/lib/export.ts` (export utilities)

## Labels
- enhancement
- gdpr
- user-management
- data-export

title: feat: Enable account deletion for users (right to erasure)
description:
## Feature Request

Allow authenticated users to permanently delete their account and all associated data (GDPR Right to Erasure).

## Motivation

Users have the legal right under GDPR Article 17 to request deletion of their personal data. The application should provide:
- Self-service account deletion
- Complete removal of personal data
- Clear confirmation flow to prevent accidental deletion
- Grace period for recovery (optional)

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want to delete my account so my personal data is removed
2. As a user, I want to confirm deletion with my password to prevent accidents
3. As a user, I want to understand what will be deleted before confirming
4. As a user, I want to receive email confirmation after account deletion

## What Gets Deleted

### User Data (Deleted)
- [ ] User account record
- [ ] Profile information (name, email)
- [ ] Linked player accounts
- [ ] Auth sessions
- [ ] Email verification tokens
- [ ] Password reset tokens

### User-Created Content (Anonymized or Deleted)
- [ ] Events where user is the sole owner → Delete
- [ ] Events with other players → Transfer to new owner or anonymize
- [ ] Player records linked to user → Anonymize (remove userId, keep name)
- [ ] Game history records → Keep but remove user association

### Preserved Data (Not Deleted)
- [ ] Events created by other users (user was just a player)
- [ ] Game history snapshots (anonymized)
- [ ] Webhook deliveries (anonymized)
- [ ] Audit logs (for compliance, anonymized)

## Proposed Solution

### API Endpoints

#### DELETE /api/users/[id]

```typescript
// Request body
{
  password: string,      // Required - verify user identity
  confirmation: string    // Must equal "DELETE MY ACCOUNT"
}

// Response
{
  ok: true,
  message: "Account deleted successfully"
}
```

### Deletion Flow

```
User clicks "Delete Account"
        ↓
Modal shows what will be deleted
        ↓
User enters password + types "DELETE MY ACCOUNT"
        ↓
API verifies password
        ↓
API checks for owned events with players
        ↓
If events have players → Show transfer options
        ↓
User confirms final deletion
        ↓
Send deletion email
        ↓
Delete account within 7 days (optional grace period)
```

### Security Considerations

1. **Password Verification**
   - Require current password to prevent accidental/hijacked deletions
   - Rate limit attempts (max 3 per hour)

2. **Confirmation String**
   - User must type "DELETE MY ACCOUNT" to confirm
   - Prevents accidental deletions from misclicks

3. **Grace Period (Optional)**
   - Keep account marked for deletion for 7 days
   - Allow user to cancel within grace period
   - After grace period, permanently delete

4. **Email Notification**
   - Send email immediately when deletion is requested
   - Include "Cancel deletion" link within grace period
   - Send final confirmation when deletion completes

5. **Session Invalidation**
   - Immediately invalidate all sessions after deletion request
   - Or keep session valid within grace period

### Event Handling

**Events with Multiple Owners or Players:**
```typescript
// Option 1: Transfer ownership to another player
// Option 2: Transfer ownership to first player joined
// Option 3: Make event public (anyone can claim)
// Option 4: Delete event only if user is sole owner
```

Recommend: **Option 4** - Only delete events where user is sole owner, otherwise transfer to next player or make public.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create API route `DELETE /api/users/[id]`
- [ ] Implement password verification
- [ ] Add confirmation string validation
- [ ] Handle event ownership transfer
- [ ] Anonymize player records
- [ ] Send deletion confirmation email
- [ ] Add "Delete Account" UI to profile
- [ ] Add i18n strings (en, pt, es, fr, de, it)
- [ ] Write tests for deletion flow
- [ ] Add audit logging
- [ ] Optional: Implement 7-day grace period

## Database Changes

```sql
-- Add deletion tracking (if implementing grace period)
ALTER TABLE "User" ADD COLUMN "deletedAt" TIMESTAMP;
ALTER TABLE "User" ADD COLUMN "deletionReason" TEXT;
```

## Test Cases

```typescript
describe("DELETE /api/users/[id]", () => {
  it("should delete account with valid password and confirmation");
  it("should reject incorrect password");
  it("should reject mismatched confirmation string");
  it("should transfer events with other players to new owner");
  it("should delete events where user is sole owner");
  it("should anonymize player records");
  it("should remove user from game history");
  it("should send deletion confirmation email");
  it("should rate limit after 3 failed attempts");
  it("should return 401 for unauthenticated users");
  it("should return 403 for deleting other users");
});
```

## Related Files
- `src/pages/api/users/[id].ts` (existing)
- `src/lib/db.server.ts` (deletion logic)
- `src/components/ProfilePage.tsx` (UI)
- `src/lib/email.ts` (notifications)

## Labels
- enhancement
- gdpr
- security
- user-management

title: feat: Enable password change for authenticated users
description:
## Feature Request

Allow authenticated users to change their password from their profile settings.

## Motivation

Users need the ability to:
- Change their password for security reasons
- Update password if they suspect compromise
- Maintain account security over time

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want to change my password so I can keep my account secure
2. As a user, I want to receive confirmation when my password is changed
3. As a user, I want to be notified via email when my password changes

## Proposed Solution

### API Endpoints

#### PUT /api/users/[id]/password

```typescript
// Request body
{
  currentPassword: string,  // Required - verify user knows current password
  newPassword: string,      // Required - min 8 characters
  confirmPassword: string   // Required - must match newPassword
}

// Response
{
  ok: true,
  message: "Password updated successfully"
}
```

### Validation
- [ ] Current password must be verified before allowing change
- [ ] New password must be at least 8 characters
- [ ] New password must match confirmation
- [ ] Rate limit: max 3 attempts per hour

### Security Considerations

1. **Verify Current Password**
   - Must provide current password to prevent session hijacking attacks
   - Use `bcrypt.compare` to verify

2. **Password Strength**
   - Minimum 8 characters
   - Optional: Password strength meter in UI

3. **Email Notification**
   - Send email notification when password is changed
   - Include "Not you?" link with revoke instructions

4. **Session Invalidation**
   - Optional: Invalidate all other sessions after password change
   - Keep current session valid

5. **Audit Logging**
   - Log password change events (without storing password)
   - Track IP address and timestamp

### UI Implementation

- [ ] Add "Change Password" section to profile page
- [ ] Form with 3 fields: current password, new password, confirm password
- [ ] Show password requirements
- [ ] Display success/error messages
- [ ] Add i18n strings for all languages

### Database Changes

No schema changes needed - using Better Auth's built-in password management.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create API route `PUT /api/users/[id]/password`
- [ ] Add rate limiting (max 3 requests per hour)
- [ ] Implement password validation
- [ ] Send email notification on password change
- [ ] Add "Change Password" UI to profile
- [ ] Add i18n strings (en, pt, es, fr, de, it)
- [ ] Write tests for password change flow
- [ ] Add audit logging

## Test Cases

```typescript
describe("PUT /api/users/[id]/password", () => {
  it("should change password with valid current password");
  it("should reject incorrect current password");
  it("should reject new password < 8 characters");
  it("should reject mismatched confirmation");
  it("should rate limit after 3 failed attempts");
  it("should send email notification");
  it("should return 401 for unauthenticated users");
  it("should return 403 for other users' passwords");
});
```

## Related Files
- `src/pages/api/users/[id].ts` (existing)
- `src/components/ProfilePage.tsx` (new or existing)
- `src/lib/email.ts` (for notifications)

## Labels
- enhancement
- security
- user-management

title: feat: Add k6 load testing infrastructure for 1000 concurrent users
description:
## Feature Request

Implement k6 load testing infrastructure to ensure the application can handle1000 concurrent users and maintain performance under load.

## Motivation

Before scaling to production with high traffic, we need to:
- Verify the application can handle 1000 concurrent users
- Identify performance bottlenecks before they affect users
- Establish baseline performance metrics
- Enable continuous performance testing in CI/CD
- Prevent performance regressions over time

## Goals

1. Set up k6 testing infrastructure
2. Create realistic load test scenarios
3. Integrate with CI/CD pipeline
4. Document performance baselines
5. Define SLOs (Service Level Objectives)

## Technical Requirements

### 1. k6 Setup
- [ ] Add k6 to project dependencies or as standalone test runner
- [ ] Create `k6/` directory structure:
  ```
  k6/
  ├── scripts/
  │   ├── smoke.js          # Quick validation (1-5 VUs)
  │   ├── load.js           # Normal load testing
  │   ├── stress.js          # Beyond normal capacity
  │   └── spike.js           # Sudden traffic spikes
  ├── config/
  │   └── thresholds.js      # Performance thresholds
  ├── lib/
  │   ├── helpers.js        # Shared test utilities
  │   └── fixtures.js       # Test data generators
  └── README.md
  ```

### 2. Test Scenarios

#### Smoke Test (Basic functionality)
- Quick validation (1-5 VUs)
- Duration: 30 seconds
- Verifies basic endpoints work under minimal load

#### Load Test (Normal conditions)
- Ramp up to 1000 VUs over 5 minutes
- Sustain for 10 minutes
- Ramp down over 2 minutes
- Total duration: ~17 minutes
- Test all critical user journeys

#### Stress Test (Beyond capacity)
- Gradually increase to 1500 VUs
- Identify breaking points
- Measure graceful degradation

#### Spike Test (Traffic bursts)
- Jump from 100 to 1000 VUs instantly
- Test auto-scaling capabilities
- Measure recovery time

### 3. Critical User Flows to Test

1. **Unauthenticated Users**
   - [ ] View public events list
   - [ ] View individual event page
   - [ ] Join event as player (anonymous)

2. **Authenticated Users**
   - [ ] Login/Signup
   - [ ] Create new event
   - [ ] Add/remove players
   - [ ] Randomize teams
   - [ ] Update event settings

3. **Real-time Features**
   - [ ] SSE connection establishment
   - [ ] Real-time player updates

4. **API Endpoints**
   - [ ] `GET /api/events/:id`
   - [ ] `POST /api/events/:id/players`
   - [ ] `POST /api/events/:id/randomize`
   - [ ] `GET /api/events/public`

### 4. Performance Thresholds (SLOs)

```javascript
// k6/config/thresholds.js
export const thresholds = {
  // http errors should be less than 1%
  http_req_failed: ['rate<0.01'],
  
  // 95% of requests should complete within 500ms
  http_req_duration: ['p(95)<500'],
  
  // 99% of requests should complete within 2s
  http_req_duration_p99: ['p(99)<2000'],
  
  // No more than 10% of requests should be slow (>1s)
  http_req_duration_slow: ['p(90)<1000'],
  
  // Connection errors should be less than0.1%
  checks: ['rate>0.99'],
};
```

### 5. Metrics to Collect

- **Response Time**: p50, p90, p95, p99 latencies
- **Throughput**: Requests per second (RPS)
- **Error Rate**: HTTP error codes, failed checks
- **Resource Usage**: CPU, memory, database connections
- **Custom Metrics**: Player join rate, team randomization time

### 6. CI/CD Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/performance.yml
name: Performance Tests
on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday at2am
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  smoke-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: grafana/k6-action@v1
        with:
          filename: k6/scripts/smoke.js
          
  load-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: grafana/k6-action@v1
        with:
          filename: k6/scripts/load.js
```

### 7. Environment Setup

- [ ] Create dedicated performance testing environment
- [ ] Configure staging database with realistic data volume
- [ ] Set up monitoring (optional: Grafana Cloud integration)
- [ ] Document environment variables needed

### 8. Data Fixtures

Generate realistic test data:
- [ ] Create 100+ events with varying player counts
- [ ] Create users with different roles (owner, player)
- [ ] Generate realistic player names and teams

### 9. Reporting

- [ ] Generate HTML reports after each run
- [ ] Compare results against baseline
- [ ] Alert on threshold violations
- [ ] Store historical results for trend analysis

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Basic Setup (Week 1)
- [ ] Install k6
- [ ] Create directory structure
- [ ] Write smoke test for core endpoints
- [ ] Run against local development environment

### Phase 2: Load Tests (Week 2)
- [ ] Write load test script
- [ ] Test authentication flows
- [ ] Test player management
- [ ] Test real-time features (SSE)

### Phase 3: CI Integration (Week 3)
- [ ] Add GitHub Actions workflow
- [ ] Configure staging environment secrets
- [ ] Set up scheduled runs
- [ ] Add PR gating for smoke tests

### Phase 4: Monitoring & Alerting (Week 4)
- [ ] Integrate with Grafana (optional)
- [ ] Set up alerting for threshold violations
- [ ] Create performance dashboard
- [ ] Document runbooks for failures

## Success Criteria

1. **Functional**: All load tests pass without errors
2. **Performance**: 
   - p95 response time < 500ms under 1000 VUs
   - Error rate < 1% under normal load
   - Zero downtime during spike tests
3. **Operational**: CI/CD integration works reliably
4. **Documentation**: Clear runbooks and guidelines

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Database connection exhaustion | Configure Prisma connection pooling |
| Rate limiting interference | Create test-specific rate limits |
| SSE connection limits | Test horizontal scaling |
| Memory leaks in long tests | Implement gradual ramp-down |
| False positives from network | Set appropriate thresholds |

## Resources

- [k6 Documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/)
- [k6 GitHub Actions](https://github.com/grafana/k6-action)
- [Performance Testing Best Practices](https://k6.io/docs/testing-guides/test-types/)
- [k6 Thresholds](https://k6.io/docs/using-k6/thresholds/)

## Related Issues

- #103 - Event archiving (performance impact on database queries)

## Labels
- enhancement
- performance
- testing

title: feat: Allow event owners to archive events
description:
## Feature Request

Allow event owners to archive events. Archived events would be hidden from the main dashboard/list but still accessible via direct link.

## Motivation

Over time, users accumulate many past events. Archiving would allow users to:
- Clean up their dashboard view
- Hide old/unwanted events while preserving them for historical records
- Focus on active/upcoming events

## Proposed Solution

### Database Changes
- Add `archived` boolean field to the Event model (default: `false`)
- Optionally add `archivedAt` timestamp field

### API Changes
- Add `PUT /api/events/[id]/archive` endpoint to toggle archive status
- Only event owner can archive/unarchive

### UI Changes
- Add "Archive" button in event settings (visible only to owner)
- Add "Unarchive" option for archived events
- Archived events:
  - Hidden from `/dashboard` and `/public` listings
  - Still accessible via direct URL `/events/[id]`
  - Show archived badge/indicator when viewing
- Add "Show archived" filter/toggle in dashboard (optional)

### Considerations
- Archived events should still:
  - Be searchable by direct URL
  - Allow players to join/unjoin (if not locked)
  - Maintain all history and data
- Default query filters should exclude archived events
- Consider adding `?archived=true` query param to view archived events

## Tasks
- [ ] Add `archived` and `archivedAt` fields to Event model
- [ ] Create migration for new fields
- [ ] Add `PUT /api/events/[id]/archive` endpoint
- [ ] Update event queries to exclude archived by default
- [ ] Add archive/unarchive button in EventPage component
- [ ] Add archived badge/indicator on archived event pages
- [ ] Update dashboard to exclude archived events
- [ ] Add i18n strings for archive-related UI text
- [ ] Add tests for archive functionality

## Labels
- enhancement

title: fix: Teams not touch-friendly - accidental team changes on scroll
description:
## Problem
Users on touch devices accidentally change player teams when scrolling. The current team selection UI is too sensitive to touch interactions.

## Expected Behavior
- Scrolling should not trigger team changes
- Team selection should require intentional action (e.g., long press, dedicated button, or swipe gesture distinct from scroll)

## Suggested Solutions
- Add touch event handling to distinguish scroll from tap
- Increase touch target area and add touch delay/debounce
- Consider a "press and hold" interaction for team changes
- Add a team selection modal/button instead of inline touch targets

## Impact
- Mobile/tablet users frequently affected
- Degrades user experience during roster management

title: Redirect to event page after login from event
description:
## Bug

When a user clicks the login button from an event page (`/events/[id]`), they are redirected to the main page (`/`) after authentication instead of back to the event they were viewing.

### Expected behavior
After logging in from an event page, the user should be redirected back to that same event page.

### Proposed fix
- Pass the current event URL as a `callbackURL` / `redirect` query parameter to the sign-in page
- After successful authentication, redirect to the stored callback URL instead of `/`

title: Allow event owner to reset player order to signup order (created_at)
description:
## Feature Request

Allow event owners to reset the player order to match the original signup order (based on `created_at` timestamps).

### Current Behavior
- Event owners can reorder players using drag-and-drop (`PUT /api/events/[id]/reorder-players`)
- Once reordered, there is no way to revert to the original signup order

### Proposed Behavior
- Add an endpoint (e.g., `POST /api/events/[id]/reset-player-order`) that allows owners to reset the `order` field of all players to match their `createdAt` timestamps
- Players would then be listed in the order they originally signed up

### Implementation Notes
- The `Player` model already has both `order` (int, reorderable) and `createdAt` (DateTime) fields
- The reset logic would:
  1. Fetch all players for the event
  2. Sort them by `createdAt`
  3. Update each player's `order` to their position in the sorted list
- This should be owner-only (same permission as `reorder-players`)

title: Events got players out of order
description:
After we enabled owner reorder an existing event got out of order without the owner did anything. 

We need to:
- replicate the issue (create event, add users and endure they follow created_at, remove the second user l, readd and then move back to the position)
- provide a system to make it clear to the users of the swaps that were done by the user (to be transparent and avoid confusions)

title: Allow to disable and manage notifications
description:
Currently we allow to subscribe to each event. This can become spammy very easily. So we need to:
- when on a subscribed event, the subscription button becomes a unsubscribe button
- on the user settings should be able to toggle notifications off for all events, or an event in specific
- should be able to disable a specific event type (EMAIL/Web notification) globally

title: Allow authenticated users to claim anonymous player entries
description:
## Problem

A common scenario: someone joins an event as an anonymous player (no account linked), then later wants to associate that player entry with their user account. This matters because:

1. **ELO attribution** — `PlayerRating` tracks ratings by name, but the `userId` field is never set for anonymous entries. Claiming the player would tie the rating history to the user's profile.
2. **Position preservation** — the player is already in the game (and possibly assigned to a team). They should not have to remove themselves and re-add just to link their account.
3. **Protection** — once claimed, the player entry becomes account-linked and can only be removed by the owner or event owner (existing logic in `DELETE /api/events/[id]/players`).

## Proposed solution

### New API endpoint: `POST /api/events/[id]/players/[playerId]/claim`

**Auth:** requires authenticated session.

**Logic:**
1. Find the player by `playerId` + `eventId`.
2. Verify the player is currently anonymous (`userId === null`). If already linked, return `409 Conflict`.
3. Verify the authenticated user does not already have another player entry linked in this event. Return `409` if so (one account = one player per event).
4. Atomically set `player.userId = session.user.id`.
5. Also update the matching `PlayerRating` record (same `eventId` + `name`) to set `userId = session.user.id`, so ELO history is attributed to the user.
6. Return `200 { ok: true }`.

### What stays the same
- Player `name`, `createdAt`, team assignment, and position in the player list are all unchanged.
- No push notifications or webhooks needed (no roster change).

### Frontend
- Show a "Claim this player" button next to anonymous players when the authenticated user has no linked player in the event.
- After claiming, the player row should show the account-linked indicator (same as when `linkToAccount` is used during add).

### Edge cases to handle
- **Name mismatch:** the user's display name may differ from the anonymous player name. The claim should keep the original player name (it's the identifier in ELO and team snapshots). Optionally allow renaming in a separate step.
- **Race condition:** use an atomic update (`updateMany` with `where: { id, userId: null }`) similar to the event ownership claim pattern in `claim.ts`.
- **Historical game snapshots:** `GameHistory.teamsSnapshot` stores player names as JSON strings. These are immutable records and should NOT be modified — ELO is already processed by name, so this is fine.

### Files to modify
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — no changes needed (Player.userId is already optional)
- `src/pages/api/events/[id]/players/[playerId]/claim.ts` — new endpoint
- `src/lib/i18n.ts` — add translation keys (`claimPlayer`, `claimPlayerDesc`, etc.)
- `src/components/` — add claim button in the player list UI
- `src/test/` — add tests for the new endpoint

### Security considerations
- Only anonymous (unlinked) players can be claimed.
- A user cannot claim a player that is already linked to another account.
- A user cannot have two linked players in the same event.
- Rate-limit the endpoint (reuse existing `rateLimitResponse`).

title: SEO for public games (structured data, meta tags, SSR)
description:
## Description
Server-render the public games page with proper meta tags and structured data so public games show up in Google.

## Requirements
- Server-side render public games page (currently client:only)
- Schema.org Event structured data (JSON-LD)
- Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags per event
- Sitemap.xml for public events
- robots.txt
- Canonical URLs

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Native mobile app (React Native or Capacitor)
description:
## Description
The API is already there. Push notifications work better natively. A native wrapper would provide a better mobile experience.

## Requirements
- Evaluate React Native vs Capacitor (Capacitor may be simpler given existing React codebase)
- Core screens: create game, event page, dashboard, public games
- Native push notifications
- Deep linking (open game links in app)
- App Store and Google Play submission
- Offline support for viewing joined games

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish (very high effort)

title: Cursor-based pagination for all list endpoints
description:
## Description
Public games, history, rankings, and dashboard all need pagination to handle growth.

## Requirements
- Cursor-based pagination (not offset) for consistency and performance
- Endpoints: /api/events/public, /api/events/:id/history, /api/events/:id/ratings, /api/me/games
- Standard response format: { data: [], nextCursor: string | null, hasMore: boolean }
- UI: infinite scroll or "Load more" button
- Default page size: 20, max: 100

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Admin dashboard (user management, analytics, moderation)
description:
## Description
For self-hosters and for the main deployment: user management, event moderation, analytics, webhook monitoring.

## Requirements
- Admin role in User model
- Admin pages: /admin/users, /admin/events, /admin/webhooks
- User management: list, search, disable/enable, view activity
- Event moderation: flag/remove inappropriate events
- Analytics: active users, events created, games played (charts)
- Webhook monitoring: delivery success rate, failing webhooks
- Protected by admin role check

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Migrate from SQLite to PostgreSQL
description:
## Description
SQLite on a Fly.io volume with 256MB RAM is a scaling ceiling. Postgres enables concurrent writes, full-text search, JSON columns, and connection pooling.

## Requirements
- Switch Prisma datasource to PostgreSQL
- Migration script for existing data
- Use Fly Postgres or Neon for hosting
- Connection pooling (PgBouncer or Prisma Accelerate)
- Update Dockerfile and fly.toml
- Update CI/CD pipeline
- Load test to verify improvement

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Real-time updates via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
description:
## Description
Replace the 5-second SWR polling with server-sent events. Player joins/leaves, team changes, and chat messages should appear instantly.

## Requirements
- SSE endpoint: GET /api/events/:id/stream
- Events: player_joined, player_left, teams_changed, comment_added, status_changed
- Client-side: replace SWR refreshInterval with EventSource
- Fallback to polling if SSE connection fails
- Connection management: heartbeat, reconnection, cleanup
- Consider scaling implications (SSE connections per server)

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Accessibility audit and improvements
description:
## Description
MUI provides a baseline but a proper audit is needed. Run axe/Lighthouse, fix issues, add ARIA labels, test with screen readers.

## Requirements
- Run Lighthouse accessibility audit on all pages
- Fix all critical and serious axe violations
- Add proper ARIA labels to interactive elements
- Keyboard navigation testing
- Screen reader testing (VoiceOver, NVDA)
- Color contrast verification in both light and dark modes
- Focus management for dialogs and dynamic content

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Add more languages (es, fr, de, it)
description:
## Description
The i18n system is clean and ready. Just needs translations for more locales.

## Requirements
- Spanish (es)
- French (fr)
- German (de)
- Italian (it)
- Language picker in the UI (currently auto-detects en/pt)
- Consider using a translation service or community contributions

## Priority
Phase 3 — Scale & Polish

title: Venue / court model for booking platform integration
description:
## Description
Add a Venue entity with address, coordinates, available courts, pricing. This bridges Convocados (player coordination) and booking apps (court reservation).

## Requirements
- New models: Venue (id, name, address, lat, lng, website, ownerId), Court (id, venueId, name, sport, pricePerHour)
- Link events to venues/courts
- Venue directory page (public)
- Venue owner dashboard
- API for venue CRUD (authenticated)
- Search venues by location/sport

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready (high effort)

title: OAuth2 + API keys for third-party integrations
description:
## Description
Third parties (booking apps, bots) need to authenticate programmatically. Add OAuth2 flow with scopes and API key management.

## Requirements
- API key model: id, userId, name, hashedKey, scopes, createdAt, lastUsedAt
- API key management UI in user profile
- Scopes: read:events, write:events, manage:players, create:events
- Bearer token authentication for API requests
- Rate limiting per API key
- Key rotation support
- OAuth2 authorization code flow for third-party apps (future)

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: iCal/CalDAV feed per user and group
description:
## Description
Per-user and per-group calendar feeds that booking apps can subscribe to for availability checking.

## Requirements
- GET /api/users/:id/calendar.ics — all upcoming games for a user
- GET /api/groups/:id/calendar.ics — all upcoming games for a group
- Standard iCal format with VEVENT entries
- Auto-updating (CalDAV-compatible)
- Private feed URLs with token authentication

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: Embeddable widget for venue/booking platforms
description:
## Description
A script tag that booking platforms can drop on their venue page: "Organize a game here" button that pre-fills location.

## Requirements
- Lightweight JS widget (<10KB)
- Configurable: venue name, location, sport preset
- Renders a button that opens Convocados create-event form with pre-filled fields
- Support iframe embed and popup modes
- Widget builder page in docs
- CORS headers for cross-origin embedding

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: Messaging integrations (Slack, Discord, WhatsApp)
description:
## Description
Post game updates to a channel, let players RSVP from chat.

## Requirements
- Slack bot: post game updates, /convocados slash command to create/join games
- Discord bot: similar to Slack
- WhatsApp Business API: send game reminders, accept RSVP replies
- Configurable per event: which channels to post to
- Two-way: actions in chat reflect in the app

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: Webhook v2: delivery logs UI, testing endpoint, improved filtering
description:
## Description
Current webhooks are functional but need polish for production integrations.

## Requirements
- Webhook delivery logs UI (show recent deliveries, status, payload, errors)
- Test endpoint: POST /api/events/:id/webhooks/:id/test — sends a test payload
- Improved event filtering: per-event-type subscription
- Configurable retry policy
- Webhook signature verification docs and example code
- Webhook health monitoring (auto-disable after N consecutive failures)

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: OpenAPI 3.1 specification
description:
## Description
Machine-readable API documentation. This is the entry point for any third-party integration.

## Requirements
- OpenAPI 3.1 spec covering all existing API routes
- Auto-serve at /api/docs or /openapi.json
- Swagger UI or Scalar for interactive documentation
- Include request/response schemas, error codes, authentication
- Keep in sync with route handlers (consider code generation or validation)

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready

title: Payment splitting (Stripe Connect)
description:
## Description
Court bookings cost money. The #1 friction point in organizing pickup games is "who's paying." This is also the natural integration point with booking platforms and the monetization path.

## Requirements
- Stripe Connect integration
- Event owner sets a total cost and split method (equal, custom)
- Players see their share and can pay via Stripe
- Payment status visible on event page
- Automatic reminders for unpaid players
- Payout to venue/organizer
- Refund handling for cancellations

## Priority
Phase 2 — Integration-Ready (high effort)

title: Email notifications (invites, reminders, weekly summary)
description:
## Description
Not everyone enables push notifications. Use the existing Resend integration to send game invites, reminders, and weekly summaries via email.

## Requirements
- Game invite email when a player is added by the owner
- Reminder emails (24h, 2h before game) — ties into scheduled reminders feature
- Weekly summary: upcoming games, recent results, ELO changes
- Unsubscribe link per email type
- Email preferences in user profile

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience

title: Replace PWA placeholder icons with real icons
description:
## Description
The PWA manifest is in place but uses 1x1 placeholder PNGs. Replace with proper branded icons.

## Requirements
- Design 192x192 and 512x512 PNG icons with the Convocados branding
- Maskable variant for Android adaptive icons
- Update public/icons/icon-192.png and public/icons/icon-512.png
- Consider adding apple-touch-icon for iOS

## Priority
Quick win

title: Player stats dashboard (cross-game stats)
description:
## Description
Show players their stats across all games: attendance rate, win rate, current streak, ELO history chart. The data already exists in PlayerRating and GameHistory.

## Requirements
- New page: /users/:id/stats or extend existing profile page
- Stats: total games, win/draw/loss ratio, attendance rate, current streak
- ELO history chart (line chart over time)
- Per-event breakdown
- Shareable stats card (OG image or screenshot-friendly layout)

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience

title: Friends / Groups (persistent player groups)
description:
## Description
Let users create persistent groups ("Tuesday Football Crew"). A group auto-populates the player list when creating a game. This is the stickiness feature.

## Requirements
- New models: Group (id, name, ownerId, createdAt), GroupMember (groupId, userId)
- API: CRUD for groups, add/remove members
- UI: group management page, group selector on CreateEventForm
- When creating a game from a group, pre-populate the player list
- Group invite link (shareable)
- Group-level notifications and calendar feed

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience (high effort)

title: Game comments / chat thread
description:
## Description
A simple comment thread per event. Not a full chat app — just enough to say "bringing extra balls" or "parking is on the left."

## Requirements
- New `Comment` model: id, eventId, authorName, userId (optional), text, createdAt
- API: GET/POST /api/events/:id/comments
- UI: collapsible comment section on EventPage
- Show author name, timestamp, and text
- Rate limit: 10 comments/min per IP
- Push notification option for new comments
- Auto-cleanup: delete comments older than 30 days

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience

title: Scheduled game reminders (push + email)
description:
## Description
The #1 reason games fall apart is people forgetting. Send reminders 24h and 2h before game time.

## Requirements
- Configurable reminder times per event (24h, 2h, 1h before)
- Push notifications to subscribed players
- Email reminders via Resend (already set up for verification)
- Cron job / scheduled task on Fly.io (use Fly Machines or a lightweight scheduler)
- Respect player notification preferences
- Include game details: title, time, location, current player count, spots left

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience

title: RSVP statuses (in / maybe / out / late)
description:
## Description
Players need to signal intent beyond binary join/leave. This reduces the constant "are you coming?" messages in WhatsApp/Discord.

## Requirements
- Add RSVP status field to Player model: `in`, `maybe`, `out`, `late`
- Default status is `in` when joining
- UI: allow players to change their status via chip/toggle on the event page
- Show status icons next to player names in the player list
- Push notification when a player changes status (especially `out` → promotes bench)
- Webhook event: `player_status_changed`

## Priority
Phase 1 — Core Experience

title: feat: require email verification on sign-up
description:
## Summary Currently, users who sign up with email/password are immediately logged in and redirected to `/` without verifying their email address. The `User.emailVerified` field exists in the schema (defaults to `false`) and the `Verification` model is already in place, but nothing enforces verification. We should require users to confirm their email before they can fully use the app. ## Current Behaviour 1. User fills in name, email, password on `/auth/signup` 2. `signUp.email()` from better-auth creates the account 3. User is immediately redirected to `/` with full access 4. `emailVerified` stays `false` forever ## Desired Behaviour 1. User signs up → account is created with `emailVerified = false` 2. A verification email is sent with a confirmation link 3. User sees a "check your email" page instead of being redirected home 4. Clicking the link sets `emailVerified = true` and redirects to `/` 5. Unverified users who try to sign in see a message asking them to verify first (or are allowed to request a new verification email) ## Implementation Plan ### 1. Configure better-auth email verification (`src/lib/auth.server.ts`) Enable the built-in `emailVerification` option in the `betterAuth()` config: ```ts export const auth = betterAuth({ database: prismaAdapter(prisma, { provider: "sqlite" }), emailAndPassword: { enabled: true, requireEmailVerification: true, }, emailVerification: { sendOnSignUp: true, autoSignInAfterVerification: true, sendVerificationEmail: async ({ user, url }) => { // send email via your provider (e.g. Resend, Nodemailer, etc.) await sendEmail({ to: user.email, subject: "Verify your email — Convocados", html: `

Click here to verify your email.

`, }); }, }, trustedOrigins: [process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL ?? "http://localhost:4321"], }); ``` ### 2. Add an email sending utility Create `src/lib/email.server.ts` with a transport (e.g. Nodemailer with SMTP, or Resend API). The choice depends on the deployment — Fly.io works well with Resend or any external SMTP. Environment variables needed: - `EMAIL_FROM` — sender address - SMTP credentials or API key for the chosen provider ### 3. Update `SignUpPage.tsx` After a successful `signUp.email()` call, instead of redirecting to `/`: ```ts // current window.location.href = "/"; // new window.location.href = "/auth/verify-email"; ``` ### 4. Create a "check your email" page Add `src/pages/auth/verify-email.astro` (and a React component) that tells the user to check their inbox. Include a "Resend verification email" button that calls `authClient.sendVerificationEmail()`. ### 5. Update `SignInPage.tsx` When `requireEmailVerification` is enabled, better-auth returns a specific error if the user has not verified. Catch this and show a helpful message with a resend link instead of the generic "Invalid email or password". ### 6. Add i18n keys (`src/lib/i18n.ts`) Add translations for both EN and PT: - `checkYourEmail` / `verificaEmail` - `verificationSent` / `emailVerificacaoEnviado` - `resendVerification` / `reenviarVerificacao` - `emailNotVerified` / `emailNaoVerificado` ### 7. Environment & deployment - Add email provider secrets to Fly.io (`fly secrets set ...`) - Add `EMAIL_FROM` to `fly.toml` `[env]` - Document the required env vars in README or `.env.development` ### 8. Tests - Unit test: mock `sendVerificationEmail` and assert it is called on sign-up - Integration test: verify that unverified users cannot sign in when `requireEmailVerification` is enabled ## Files to modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/lib/auth.server.ts` | Enable `requireEmailVerification`, add `emailVerification` config | | `src/lib/email.server.ts` | **New** — email sending utility | | `src/components/SignUpPage.tsx` | Redirect to verify-email page | | `src/components/SignInPage.tsx` | Handle unverified-email error | | `src/pages/auth/verify-email.astro` | **New** — check-your-email page | | `src/lib/i18n.ts` | Add new translation keys | | `fly.toml` | Add `EMAIL_FROM` env var | | `.env.development` | Document email env vars | ## Dependencies An email provider is required. Options: - **Resend** (recommended — simple API, free tier of 100 emails/day) - Nodemailer + any SMTP provider - AWS SES ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Sign-up sends a verification email - [ ] Unverified users cannot sign in (shown a clear message) - [ ] Clicking the verification link marks the user as verified and signs them in - [ ] "Resend verification" button works from the verify-email page - [ ] i18n translations for EN and PT - [ ] Env vars documented - [ ] Tests cover the happy path and unverified sign-in attempt

title: feat: add passwordless email login (magic link)
description:
## Summary

Add support for passwordless email login using magic links via better-auth's `magicLink` plugin.

## Motivation

Some users prefer not to create/remember passwords. Magic link login sends a one-time link to the user's email, which authenticates them on click. This is a common pattern for casual apps where friction should be minimal.

## Implementation Notes

- better-auth has a built-in `magic-link` plugin: `import { magicLink } from 'better-auth/plugins/magic-link'`
- Requires an email transport (e.g. Resend, Nodemailer, or a custom `sendEmail` function)
- Add a "Sign in with email" option on the sign-in page alongside the existing email/password form
- Client-side: `authClient.signIn.magicLink({ email })`
- Consider adding the plugin to the existing better-auth config in `src/lib/auth.server.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User can enter their email and receive a magic link
- [ ] Clicking the link authenticates the user and redirects to the app
- [ ] Works for both new and existing users
- [ ] Email transport configured (env var for API key)
- [ ] i18n strings for magic link flow in EN and PT

title: Public events: add filters (sport, availability) and views (cards, table, map)
description:
## Summary

Enhance the public events page (`/public`) with filtering capabilities and multiple view modes so users can find games that match their preferences more easily.

## Current state

- `PublicGamesPage.tsx` renders a flat grid of cards with no filtering
- `GET /api/events/public` returns all public events ordered by date
- No `sport` field exists on the Event model
- Location is stored as free text (no coordinates)

## Proposed changes

### 1. Add `sport` field to Event model

Add a `sport` column (string, default `"football-5v5"`) to the `Event` schema. Expose it in the create form as a select with common options. Include it in the public API response.

```prisma
model Event {
  ...
  sport  String  @default("football-5v5")
  ...
}
```

### 2. Sport presets and default maxPlayers

Each sport option carries a default `maxPlayers` value. When the user selects or changes the sport, `maxPlayers` is automatically set to the sport default. The user can still manually override it afterward, but any subsequent sport change resets it again.

Proposed presets:

| Sport | Default maxPlayers |
|---|---|
| Football 5v5 | 10 |
| Football 7v7 | 14 |
| Football 11v11 | 22 |
| Futsal | 10 |
| Basketball | 10 |
| Volleyball | 12 |
| Tennis (singles) | 2 |
| Tennis (doubles) | 4 |
| Padel | 4 |
| Other | 10 |

This mapping should live in a shared constant (e.g. `src/lib/sports.ts`) so it can be reused across the create form, event page, and filters.

### 3. Update forms to support sport selection

- **Create form** (`CreateEventForm.tsx`): Add a sport selector (dropdown or chip group). When the sport changes, auto-set `maxPlayers` to the sport default. The user can still manually adjust `maxPlayers` after selection.
- **Event page** (`EventPage.tsx`): Add the ability to view and update the sport for an existing event (e.g. an inline edit or a settings section). Changing the sport here should also reset `maxPlayers` to the new sport default (with a confirmation if teams are already generated). This requires a new API endpoint (e.g. `PUT /api/events/[id]/sport`) or extending an existing update endpoint.

### 4. Filters

Add a filter bar above the events list with:

- **Sport** — dropdown/chip selector to filter by sport type
- **Has spots** — toggle to hide full events (`spotsLeft > 0`)

Filters should be applied client-side (the dataset is small) and persisted in URL query params so links are shareable.

### 5. View modes

Add a toggle group (icons) to switch between three views:

#### Cards (default, current behavior)
Keep the existing `Grid2` card layout.

#### Table
A compact `Table` view with columns: title, sport, location, date/time, players (e.g. `8/10`), status chip (full/spots left), and a join button.

#### Map
- On first load, request the browser Geolocation API to center the map on the user
- Use a free tile provider (e.g. OpenStreetMap via `react-leaflet` or a lightweight `<iframe>` embed)
- Geocode event locations to lat/lng — either:
  - (a) Store `latitude`/`longitude` on the Event model (filled at creation via a geocoding API), or
  - (b) Geocode client-side on render using Nominatim (simpler, no schema change, but rate-limited)
- Each event is a map pin; clicking opens a popup with event details and a join link
- Fall back gracefully if geolocation is denied (default to a wide zoom or a known region)

### 6. API changes

Update `GET /api/events/public` response to include the new `sport` field. No server-side filtering needed initially — client-side is fine for the expected volume.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create `src/lib/sports.ts` with sport presets (id, label, defaultMaxPlayers)
- [ ] Add `sport` field to Prisma schema + migration
- [ ] Add sport selector to `CreateEventForm` with auto-set of `maxPlayers` on change
- [ ] Add sport display + edit to `EventPage` (with API endpoint), resetting `maxPlayers` on change
- [ ] Update `GET /api/events/public` to return `sport`
- [ ] Build filter bar component (sport dropdown, "has spots" toggle)
- [ ] Implement table view
- [ ] Implement map view (leaflet or similar)
- [ ] Handle geolocation permission + fallback
- [ ] Add i18n keys for new UI strings (sport names, filter labels, view labels)
- [ ] Persist view mode and filters in URL query params
- [ ] Consider storing lat/lng on Event for reliable map pins

## Design notes

- View toggle should use `ToggleButtonGroup` from MUI with icons (GridView, TableRows, Map)
- Filter bar and view toggle sit in a single row between the page header and the events list
- Map view should be lazy-loaded to avoid bundling leaflet for users who never switch to it
- Mobile: filters collapse into a single "Filter" button that opens a bottom sheet or dialog
- Sport selector in the create form should visually indicate the resulting `maxPlayers` (e.g. "Football 5v5 (10 players)")

title: Documentation Site: Full Docs with Navbar, Guides, API Reference & Tutorials
description:
## Problem

Convocados has no documentation. The README is outdated (references yarn and Jest, neither of which are used anymore). New users, contributors, and developers integrating via the webhook API have no guidance on how to use, deploy, or extend the app.

## Solution

Build a full documentation site served from the app itself (at `/docs`), using Astro content collections or static pages. Add a "Docs" link to the existing navbar so it is always accessible. The docs should be written in Markdown for easy extensibility, rendered with a clean sidebar layout.

## Docs Structure

```
/docs                        → Overview / landing
/docs/quickstart             → Get running in 5 minutes
/docs/tutorial               → Step-by-step: create a game, add players, randomize
/docs/deployment             → Deploy to Fly.io (and Docker in general)
/docs/features               → Feature overview
/docs/features/events        → Creating & managing events
/docs/features/players       → Adding/removing players, bench system
/docs/features/teams         → Randomizing, drag-and-drop, renaming
/docs/features/recurrence    → Recurring games, auto-reset
/docs/features/notifications → Push notifications setup
/docs/features/history       → Game history & scores
/docs/api                    → API reference overview
/docs/api/events             → POST /api/events, GET /api/events/[id]
/docs/api/players            → POST/DELETE /api/events/[id]/players
/docs/api/teams              → POST randomize, PUT teams, PUT team-names
/docs/api/webhooks           → Webhook CRUD + payload format + HMAC signing
/docs/api/push               → Push subscription endpoints
/docs/guides/webhook-openclaw → Guide: integrate with OpenClaw via webhooks
/docs/guides/self-hosting    → Guide: self-host with Docker + SQLite
/docs/contributing           → Dev setup, running tests, project structure
```

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Infrastructure and Navbar

- [ ] Add Docs link to the navbar in ResponsiveLayout.tsx (icon + text, next to GitHub icon)
- [ ] Add i18n strings for docs navigation (docs, documentation)
- [ ] Create /docs Astro layout with sidebar navigation
  - Sidebar with collapsible sections (Getting Started, Features, API, Guides)
  - Mobile-responsive: hamburger menu or drawer for sidebar
  - Breadcrumbs for navigation context
  - Previous/Next page links at the bottom
- [ ] Set up Markdown rendering for doc pages (Astro content collections or MDX)
- [ ] Style docs to match the existing MUI theme (dark/light mode support)

### Phase 2: Getting Started

- [ ] Overview (/docs): What is Convocados, key features, screenshot
- [ ] Quickstart (/docs/quickstart): Prerequisites, clone, install, env vars, run dev, create first game
- [ ] Tutorial (/docs/tutorial): End-to-end walkthrough — create event, share link, players join, randomize, view history

### Phase 3: Feature Docs

- [ ] Events: Creating events, event settings (max players, team names), recurring events
- [ ] Players: Adding/removing, bench system, auto-promotion, known players/autocomplete
- [ ] Teams: Randomize, drag-and-drop reorder, rename teams
- [ ] Recurrence: How recurring games work, reset behavior, history snapshots
- [ ] Notifications: Push notification setup, VAPID keys, browser support
- [ ] History: Game history, scores, editable window

### Phase 4: API Reference

- [ ] Overview (/docs/api): Base URL, content type, error format, rate limiting
- [ ] Events API: POST /api/events, GET /api/events/[id], GET /api/events/[id]/status
- [ ] Players API: POST /api/events/[id]/players, DELETE /api/events/[id]/players
- [ ] Teams API: POST /api/events/[id]/randomize, PUT /api/events/[id]/teams, PUT /api/events/[id]/team-names
- [ ] Webhooks API: Full CRUD, payload format, event types, HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, retry behavior, delivery status
- [ ] Push API: POST/DELETE /api/events/[id]/push, GET /api/push/vapid-public-key
- [ ] Known Players API: GET /api/events/[id]/known-players
- [ ] History API: GET/PUT /api/events/[id]/history

### Phase 5: Guides

- [ ] Webhook + OpenClaw Integration: Step-by-step guide to register a webhook pointing at OpenClaw, example payloads, HMAC verification
- [ ] Self-Hosting: Docker build, SQLite volume mount, env vars, reverse proxy setup
- [ ] Contributing: Dev setup, project structure overview, running tests, coverage thresholds, PR guidelines

### Phase 6: Polish

- [ ] Update README.md to reflect current stack and link to /docs
- [ ] Add search functionality to docs (client-side text search)
- [ ] Add code copy buttons to all code blocks
- [ ] Ensure all docs pages have proper title and meta descriptions
- [ ] Test dark/light mode across all doc pages

## Technical Decisions

- Serve from the app itself at /docs — no separate site to maintain
- Markdown/MDX content — easy for contributors to edit and extend
- Astro content collections — type-safe frontmatter, automatic slug generation
- Sidebar layout — standard docs pattern (like Astro docs, Next.js docs)
- Reuse existing theme — MUI dark/light mode, same fonts and colors
- i18n ready — docs structure supports future pt translations

## Navbar Change

Add a small Docs button/link to the existing ResponsiveLayout AppBar, between the app name and the dark mode toggle.

On mobile, Docs can be an icon-only button to save space.

## Priority

This is foundational for onboarding new users and enabling third-party integrations (especially the webhook/OpenClaw flow). The API reference for webhooks is the most urgent section since the feature just shipped without docs.

title: Implement Webhook System for Event Notifications
description:
## Problem

Currently, event notifications (player joined, player left, game full, etc.) are only delivered via web push notifications to browsers. External systems cannot subscribe to event changes without implementing their own polling mechanism.

## Solution

Implement a simple but resilient webhook system that allows external systems to register webhook endpoints and receive HTTP POST callbacks when events occur.

## API Design

### Subscribe to Event Webhooks

```
POST /api/events/[id]/webhooks
{
  "url": "https://example.com/webhook",
  "events": ["player_joined", "player_left", "game_full", "game_reset"] // optional, defaults to all
  "secret": "optional-hmac-secret" // for signature verification
}

Response:
{
  "id": "wh_xxx",
  "url": "https://example.com/webhook",
  "events": [...],
  "secret": "redacted"
}
```

### Unsubscribe

```
DELETE /api/events/[id]/webhooks/[webhookId]
```

### List Subscriptions

```
GET /api/events/[id]/webhooks

Response:
{
  "webhooks": [{ "id": "wh_xxx", "url": "...", "events": [...] }]
}
```

## Webhook Payload

```json
{
  "event": "player_joined",
  "eventId": "evt_xxx",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "data": {
    "playerName": "Carlos",
    "isActive": true,
    "spotsLeft": 3
  },
  "signature": "sha256=<hmac-sha256>" // if secret configured
}
```

## Resilience Features

1. **Retry with exponential backoff**: Failed deliveries retry at 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s intervals, up to 5 attempts
2. **Delivery logging**: Store last delivery status and timestamp for debugging
3. **Timeout**: 5-second timeout per request to prevent hanging
4. **Signature verification**: HMAC-SHA256 signature when secret is configured
5. **Idempotency**: Include unique event ID in payload for deduplication on receiver side

## Database Schema

```prisma
model WebhookSubscription {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  eventId   String
  event     Event    @relation(...)
  url       String
  secret    String?  // hashed
  events    String   // JSON array
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([eventId, url])
}

model WebhookDelivery {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  webhookId    String
  webhook      WebhookSubscription @relation(...)
  eventId      String
  eventType    String
  payload      String   // JSON
  status       String   // "pending", "success", "failed"
  attempts     Int      @default(0)
  lastAttempt  DateTime?
  deliveredAt  DateTime?
  error        String?
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
}
```

## Implementation Tasks

- [ ] Add `WebhookSubscription` and `WebhookDelivery` models to schema
- [ ] Create `POST /api/events/[id]/webhooks` endpoint
- [ ] Create `GET /api/events/[id]/webhooks` endpoint
- [ ] Create `DELETE /api/events/[id]/webhooks/[webhookId]` endpoint
- [ ] Create webhook delivery worker/service
- [ ] Integrate webhook triggers into existing event handlers (player join/leave/etc.)
- [ ] Add retry logic with exponential backoff
- [ ] Add signature generation for secure webhooks
- [ ] Add i18n strings (optional: UI for managing webhooks)
- [ ] Add tests for webhook delivery

## Notes

- Keep it simple: no UI required initially, just API endpoints
- Single webhook worker processes deliveries asynchronously
- Clean up old `WebhookDelivery` records after 7 days
- Consider rate limiting webhook subscriptions per event (e.g., max 10)

title: Smart player name suggestions from history (autocomplete + quick-tap)
description:
## Problem

Players type their name every time they join a recurring event. On mobile this leads to:
- Typos and inconsistencies ("Zé" vs "ze" vs "José" vs "jose")
- Slow input on small keyboards
- Duplicate identities that break future features like ELO rankings (#29)

The app already has all the data needed to solve this — `GameHistory.teamsSnapshot` contains every player name that ever participated in the event — but it is not surfaced during the join flow.

## Solution: Two-layer name suggestions

### Layer 1: Quick-tap chips (zero typing)

Show tappable name chips above the input field, sourced from historical players who are **not already in the current player list**. On mobile, one tap = joined. No keyboard needed.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Join this game                     │
│                                     │
│  Recent players:                    │
│  [Carlos] [Ana] [Zé] [Miguel] ...  │  ← tap to join instantly
│                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐ ┌──────┐  │
│  │ Your name            │ │ Join │  │  ← still available for new names
│  └─────────────────────┘ └──────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Data source:** `GET /api/events/[id]/known-players` — returns deduplicated names from all `GameHistory.teamsSnapshot` entries for this event, sorted by frequency (most frequent first). Excludes names already in the current player list.

**Ordering priority:**
1. The name stored in `localStorage` (`qj_name`) — shown first/highlighted if not already joined
2. Names sorted by participation count (most games played → first)

### Layer 2: Autocomplete on the text field

When the user starts typing in either the Quick Join field or the admin add-player field, show a filtered dropdown of known names matching the input. This catches partial matches and prevents typos.

- Filter is case-insensitive and accent-insensitive (normalize with `String.normalize("NFD").replace(/[\u0300-\u036f]/g, "")`)
- Show matches as a simple dropdown list below the input
- Tapping a suggestion fills the input and submits immediately
- If no match, the user can still type a new name freely (no blocking)

### Layer 3: Device memory (localStorage)

Expand `localStorage` from storing a single `qj_name` to storing a list of recently used names across events on this device:

```typescript
// localStorage key: "known_names"
// Value: ["Carlos", "Ana", "Zé"] — last 20 names used from this device
```

These names appear as suggestions even on brand-new events where there is no history yet. They are merged with server-side suggestions (server names take priority for ordering, device names fill gaps).

---

## API Changes

### New: `GET /api/events/[id]/known-players`

Returns deduplicated player names extracted from all `GameHistory` entries for this event.

```typescript
// Response
{
  "players": [
    { "name": "Carlos", "gamesPlayed": 15 },
    { "name": "Ana", "gamesPlayed": 12 },
    { "name": "Zé", "gamesPlayed": 8 }
  ]
}
```

**Implementation:**
1. Query all `GameHistory` where `eventId = id` and `status = "played"`
2. Parse each `teamsSnapshot` JSON
3. Extract all player names, count occurrences
4. Exclude names already in the current `Player` list for this event
5. Sort by count descending
6. Return top 30

This is a read-only endpoint, no auth needed.

---

## UI Changes

### Quick Join component (`EventPage.tsx` → `QuickJoin`)

**When user has NOT joined yet:**
- Fetch `/api/events/[id]/known-players` on mount
- Merge with `localStorage` known names
- Show chips for top ~8 names (responsive — fewer on narrow screens)
- "Show all" expander if more than 8
- Tapping a chip calls `onJoin(name)` directly — one tap join
- Highlight the `localStorage` `qj_name` chip if present (e.g., outlined primary color)
- Below chips, keep the existing text field with autocomplete dropdown added

**When user HAS joined:**
- No change — show the current "You joined as X" / Leave button

### Admin add-player field (main player section)

- Add autocomplete dropdown to the existing `TextField`
- Same data source (known players from history + localStorage)
- Dropdown appears on focus and filters as user types
- Accent-insensitive matching
- Selecting a suggestion submits it

### Mobile considerations
- Chips must be large enough for touch targets (min 44px height per WCAG)
- Horizontal scroll for chips row if many names (no wrapping to avoid pushing content down)
- Autocomplete dropdown should appear above the keyboard, not behind it
- Use `inputMode="text"` and `autoCapitalize="words"` on the name field

---

## Implementation Tasks

- [ ] Create `GET /api/events/[id]/known-players` endpoint
- [ ] Expand localStorage to store list of recently used names (`known_names`, max 20)
- [ ] Update `QuickJoin` component: add quick-tap chips from known players
- [ ] Update `QuickJoin` component: add autocomplete dropdown to text field
- [ ] Update admin add-player `TextField`: add autocomplete dropdown
- [ ] Add accent-insensitive matching utility function
- [ ] Add i18n strings (`recentPlayers`, `showAll`, `noSuggestionsFound`)
- [ ] Save name to `known_names` localStorage list on successful join
- [ ] Test on mobile (touch targets, keyboard interaction, scroll behavior)
- [ ] Test with empty history (new event — only localStorage suggestions)
- [ ] Test with large history (30+ unique players)

## i18n Strings

```typescript
// English
recentPlayers: "Recent players",
showAllPlayers: "Show all",
noSuggestions: "Type a new name",

// Portuguese
recentPlayers: "Jogadores recentes",
showAllPlayers: "Ver todos",
noSuggestions: "Escreve um novo nome",
```

---

## Relationship to Other Issues

- **Prerequisite for #29 (ELO):** Consistent player names are critical for accurate ELO tracking. This feature reduces name fragmentation at the source.
- **Independent of #27/#28 (Auth):** Works entirely without authentication. When auth lands, authenticated users could have their canonical name auto-filled, but this feature stands alone.

## Notes

- The endpoint parses `teamsSnapshot` JSON server-side. This field is already populated by the recurring event reset logic, so all recurring events with history will have data.
- For non-recurring events with no history, the feature gracefully degrades to localStorage-only suggestions (or no suggestions for first-time users — just the plain text field as today).
- No fuzzy deduplication on the server (e.g., merging "Zé" and "ze") — that is a separate concern. This feature prevents the problem at input time rather than fixing it after the fact.
- Consider adding a "Not you? Pick a different name" link when the localStorage name is auto-highlighted, so users sharing a device can easily switch.

title: ELO Ranking System with Player Identity Resolution
description:
## Overview

Add an ELO-based ranking system that uses match history to rate players and produce better-balanced team recommendations. The system must work **without requiring authentication**, preserving the current frictionless join experience, while optionally benefiting from auth when available (issues #27/#28).

## Problem

Currently, `Randomize()` uses a pure Fisher-Yates shuffle — teams are completely random regardless of player skill. Over time, recurring groups accumulate match history (scores, team compositions) in `GameHistory` that goes unused for balancing.

## Design Principles

1. **Zero-auth by default** — ELO works purely on name-based identity within an event group
2. **Auth is an enhancement, not a requirement** — if #27/#28 land, authenticated users get cross-event identity for free
3. **Seamless** — no new screens or flows for basic usage; rankings appear naturally in the history/event pages
4. **Transparent** — players can see ratings and understand why teams were suggested

---

## Identity Strategy (No Separate Auth System Needed)

### Tier 1: Event-scoped name identity (works today, no auth)

Players within a recurring event are already uniquely identified by `@@unique([eventId, name])`. Since ELO only matters within a recurring group (the same people playing together weekly), **event-scoped name matching is sufficient**:

- Parse `GameHistory.teamsSnapshot` JSON to extract player names per historical match
- Match names case-insensitively within the same event
- Store ELO ratings scoped to `(eventId, playerName)`

This covers the primary use case: a group of friends who play together regularly under the same recurring event.

### Tier 2: Authenticated cross-event identity (future, after #27/#28)

When auth lands, optionally link a player name to a user account:
- A logged-in user joining an event claims that name → links `PlayerRating.userId`
- Cross-event ELO aggregation becomes possible (same person in multiple groups)
- Name conflicts are resolved (two "João" in different events are disambiguated)

**No new auth system is needed.** The existing better-auth issues (#27/#28) cover this. The ELO schema just includes an optional `userId` FK that remains null until auth exists.

---

## Database Schema Changes

```prisma
model PlayerRating {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  eventId   String
  event     Event    @relation(fields: [eventId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  name      String                    // player name (case-insensitive match)
  userId    String?                   // optional FK to User table (when auth exists)
  rating    Float    @default(1000)   // ELO rating
  gamesPlayed Int    @default(0)
  wins      Int      @default(0)
  draws     Int      @default(0)
  losses    Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  @@unique([eventId, name])
  @@index([userId])
}
```

Add relation to Event:
```prisma
model Event {
  // ... existing fields
  playerRatings PlayerRating[]
}
```

---

## ELO Calculation

### Algorithm
- Standard ELO with K-factor = 32 (adjustable)
- Higher K-factor (K=40) for players with < 10 games (provisional period)
- Team ELO = average of team members' individual ELOs
- Expected score: `E = 1 / (1 + 10^((opponentElo - playerElo) / 400))`
- Win = 1.0, Draw = 0.5, Loss = 0.0
- New rating: `R' = R + K * (S - E)`

### When scores are recorded
After a `GameHistory` entry gets a score update (via PATCH), recalculate ELO:
- Determine outcome: team with higher score wins, equal = draw
- Update each player's `PlayerRating` for that event
- Track which history entries have been processed (add `eloProcessed Boolean @default(false)` to `GameHistory`)

### Backfill
- Add a one-time migration endpoint/script that processes all existing `GameHistory` entries with scores, in chronological order, to bootstrap ratings

---

## API Endpoints

### `GET /api/events/[id]/ratings`
Returns all player ratings for an event, sorted by rating descending.
```json
[
  { "name": "Carlos", "rating": 1087, "gamesPlayed": 12, "wins": 7, "draws": 2, "losses": 3 },
  { "name": "Ana", "rating": 1045, "gamesPlayed": 10, "wins": 6, "draws": 1, "losses": 3 }
]
```

### `POST /api/events/[id]/randomize` (modify existing)
Add optional query param `?balanced=true`:
- When `balanced=true`: use ELO-weighted team balancing instead of pure random
- Algorithm: sort players by ELO, then alternate-pick (snake draft) or use greedy balancing to minimize team ELO difference
- Default remains pure random (backward compatible)

### `POST /api/events/[id]/ratings/recalculate`
Admin/manual trigger to reprocess all history and rebuild ratings from scratch.

---

## UI Changes

### History Page (`HistoryPage.tsx`)
- Show player ELO change per match (e.g., "+12" / "-8" next to each player name in the team snapshot)

### Event Page (`EventPage.tsx`)
- Add "Balanced" toggle next to the "Randomize Teams" button
- When toggled on, randomize uses ELO-weighted balancing
- Show small ELO badge next to player names in the player list (optional, can be toggled)

### New: Ratings/Leaderboard section
- Accessible from event page (tab or link next to History)
- Simple table: rank, name, rating, W/D/L, games played
- Minimal — no separate page needed initially, can be a collapsible section

---

## Implementation Tasks

- [ ] Add `PlayerRating` model to Prisma schema + `eloProcessed` field to `GameHistory`
- [ ] Run migration
- [ ] Create `src/lib/elo.server.ts` with ELO calculation functions
- [ ] Create `GET /api/events/[id]/ratings` endpoint
- [ ] Modify `PATCH /api/events/[id]/history/[historyId]` to trigger ELO recalculation when score is saved
- [ ] Create `POST /api/events/[id]/ratings/recalculate` endpoint for backfill
- [ ] Modify `POST /api/events/[id]/randomize` to support `?balanced=true` with ELO-weighted balancing
- [ ] Add ratings display to History page (ELO deltas per match)
- [ ] Add "Balanced teams" toggle to Event page
- [ ] Add leaderboard section to Event page
- [ ] Add i18n strings for new UI elements
- [ ] Write tests for ELO calculation logic
- [ ] Write tests for balanced team generation
- [ ] Backfill script for existing history data

## Dependencies

- None for Tier 1 (name-based identity)
- Issues #27/#28 for Tier 2 (cross-event identity via `userId`)

## Notes

- ELO ratings are scoped per event, not global — this matches how the app is used (recurring groups)
- No auth is needed for the core feature. The `userId` column is nullable and only becomes useful after #27/#28
- The balanced randomizer should still have randomness — it's not a strict optimal split, but a "good enough" balance with some shuffle
- K-factor and starting rating (1000) should be configurable constants, not hardcoded everywhere
- Consider showing a "confidence" indicator for players with few games (provisional rating)

title: Implement Google OAuth Authentication with better-auth
description:
## Overview
Add Google OAuth authentication using [better-auth](https://better-auth.com/) library as an alternative sign-in method.

## Requirements
- Add Google OAuth as a social login provider
- Integrate with existing better-auth setup from password auth
- Keep implementation as simple as possible
- Use SQLite database (via Prisma) for user storage

## Prerequisites
- Email/password authentication must be implemented first (see related issue)
- Google Cloud Console project set up

## Technical Details

### Google Cloud Console Setup
1. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials in Google Cloud Console
2. Configure OAuth consent screen
3. Add authorized redirect URIs:
   - Development: `http://localhost:4321/api/auth/callback/google`
   - Production: `https://your-domain.com/api/auth/callback/google`
4. Required scopes: `profile`, `email`

### Environment Variables Needed
```env
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-cloud-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-cloud-console>
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=<random-secret-key>
BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:4321
```

### Implementation Tasks
- [ ] Set up Google Cloud Console project and OAuth credentials
- [ ] Add Google provider to better-auth config
- [ ] Add "Sign in with Google" button to sign-in page
- [ ] Add "Sign up with Google" button to sign-up page
- [ ] Handle OAuth callback route
- [ ] Test Google sign-in flow
- [ ] Test Google sign-up flow
- [ ] Test linking Google account to existing email account
- [ ] Handle edge cases (user denies access, etc.)

## Code Example
```typescript
// lib/auth.ts
import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";

export const auth = betterAuth({
    baseURL: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL,
    socialProviders: {
        google: {
            clientId: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID as string,
            clientSecret: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET as string,
        },
    },
});
```

```typescript
// Client-side
import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/client";

const authClient = createAuthClient();

const handleGoogleSignIn = async () => {
    await authClient.signIn.social({
        provider: "google",
        callbackURL: "/dashboard",
    });
};
```

## Success Criteria
- Users can sign in with Google
- Users can sign up with Google
- Google profile information (name, email, avatar) is captured
- Session is created successfully after OAuth flow
- Works alongside email/password authentication

## Resources
- [better-auth Google OAuth Documentation](https://better-auth.com/docs/providers/google)
- [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
- [better-auth Social Providers](https://better-auth.com/docs/concepts/social)

## Notes
- Google OAuth should be optional, not required
- Users should be able to use either Google or email/password
- Consider account linking strategy (same email = same account?)
- Keep implementation simple and focused

title: Implement Email/Password Authentication with better-auth
description:
## Overview
Implement email/password authentication using [better-auth](https://better-auth.com/) library with a role-based permission model for events.

## Roles

Three user types interact with events:

| Role | Identity | How it works |
|------|----------|-------------|
| **Anonymous** | None — name string only | Current behavior, anyone with the link |
| **Authenticated** | `userId` on Player record | Email/password account via better-auth |
| **Owner** | `ownerId` on Event record | The authenticated user who created or claimed the event |

## Permission Matrix

| Action | Anonymous | Authenticated | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Create event | ✅ ownerless | ✅ becomes owner | — |
| View event | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Share event | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Edit event details** (see below) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Claim ownership of ownerless event | ❌ | ✅ first come | — |
| Transfer ownership | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ to any authenticated player |
| Add self as player | ✅ name, unprotected | ✅ account-linked, protected | ✅ |
| Remove self | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Remove unprotected player | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Remove protected player | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Randomize teams | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Drag-drop edit teams | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Edit history/scores | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Manage webhooks | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Subscribe to notifications | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| View personal cross-event history | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |

### "Edit event details" — owner-only endpoints

These endpoints modify event configuration and should require ownership:

| Endpoint | Action |
|---|---|
| `PUT /api/events/[id]/location` | Change location (triggers geocoding) |
| `PUT /api/events/[id]/sport` | Change sport (resets maxPlayers) |
| `PUT /api/events/[id]/balanced` | Toggle ELO-balanced team mode |
| `PUT /api/events/[id]/visibility` | Toggle public/private |
| `PUT /api/events/[id]/team-names` | Rename teams |
| `PATCH /api/events/[id]/history/[historyId]` | Edit game scores |
| `POST/DELETE /api/events/[id]/webhooks` | Manage webhook subscriptions |

Note: `PUT /api/events/[id]/teams` (save team assignments after drag-drop) and `POST /api/events/[id]/randomize` remain open to all users — these are collaborative actions.

## Ownership Model

- **Authenticated creates event** → stored as `ownerId` on Event
- **Anonymous creates event** → `ownerId = null` (ownerless)
- **Claim**: any authenticated user can claim an ownerless event (first come, first served)
- **Transfer**: owner can transfer ownership to another authenticated user who is a player in the event

## Player Identity

- Anonymous players: name strings with no `userId` — unprotected, anyone can remove
- Authenticated players: Player record gets a `userId` FK — protected, only self or owner can remove
- Protected status is visible in the UI (e.g. a small icon on the player chip)

## Authenticated User Benefits

- Name protection — cannot be removed by non-owners
- Persistent identity across events — no name ambiguity
- Personal dashboard: "My games" listing all joined events
- Cross-event history and stats

## Requirements

- Install and configure better-auth library
- Use SQLite database via Prisma for user storage
- Support email/password sign up and sign in
- No email verification initially
- No password reset initially

## Technical Details

### Dependencies
- `better-auth` — core authentication library
- Better-auth SQLite adapter (compatible with Prisma)

### Environment Variables
```env
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=<random-secret-key>
BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:4321
```

### Database Changes
- Better-auth tables: `user`, `session`, `account`
- Add `ownerId` (nullable FK → user) to `Event` table
- Add `userId` (nullable FK → user) to `Player` table

### Implementation Tasks
- [ ] Install better-auth and SQLite adapter
- [ ] Create auth configuration in `lib/auth.ts`
- [ ] Set up auth API route at `/api/auth/*`
- [ ] Create sign up form — email, password, confirm password
- [ ] Create sign in form — email, password
- [ ] Add auth client-side helpers
- [ ] Session management — persist across reloads
- [ ] Add `ownerId` to Event schema + migration
- [ ] Add `userId` to Player schema + migration
- [ ] Enforce permission matrix on owner-only API routes:
  - [ ] `PUT /api/events/[id]/location`
  - [ ] `PUT /api/events/[id]/sport`
  - [ ] `PUT /api/events/[id]/balanced`
  - [ ] `PUT /api/events/[id]/visibility`
  - [ ] `PUT /api/events/[id]/team-names`
  - [ ] `PATCH /api/events/[id]/history/[historyId]`
  - [ ] `POST/DELETE /api/events/[id]/webhooks`
- [ ] Enforce player removal permissions (protected vs unprotected)
- [ ] Show protected player indicator in UI
- [ ] Hide owner-only controls from non-owners in EventPage
- [ ] Claim ownership flow for ownerless events
- [ ] Transfer ownership flow — owner to authenticated player
- [ ] Personal "My games" dashboard page
- [ ] Migration: existing events get `ownerId = null`, existing players get `userId = null`

## UI Changes

- **AppBar**: Add sign in / sign up / user menu
- **EventPage**: Hide edit controls (location, sport, balanced, visibility, team names, webhooks, scores) from non-owners. Show "Claim ownership" button for authenticated users on ownerless events.
- **Player chips**: Show lock/shield icon on protected (authenticated) players
- **New pages**: `/auth/signin`, `/auth/signup`, `/dashboard` (my games)

## Success Criteria
- Anonymous users experience zero changes from current behavior
- Authenticated users can sign up, sign in, and are protected from removal
- Event owners can manage event details, remove any player, edit scores
- Ownership can be claimed on ownerless events and transferred between authenticated users
- Personal game history works across events

## Resources
- [better-auth Documentation](https://better-auth.com/)
- [better-auth SQLite Adapter](https://better-auth.com/docs/adapters/sqlite)
